facts.text: New Britain earned the nickname “Hardware City” because 19th‑century firms like Stanley Works and Corbin Locks made it a manufacturing hub.
The Stanley Works began in 1843 when Frederick Trent Stanley founded a bolt manufactory that later became Stanley Black & Decker.
Central Connecticut State University, founded as a normal school in 1849, sits in New Britain and shares the city’s historic industrial roots.
The New Britain Museum of American Art, established in 1903, houses a celebrated collection of American paintings and reflects the city’s artistic heritage.
Walnut Hill Park, designed by Frederick Law Olmsted, offers a green oasis and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
The New Britain Bees, a Double‑A baseball team, play at Willow Brook Park, where fans can enjoy a hotdog while watching the game.
